All right, you're back pretty cool stuff, right? I mean, this is where the Rubber Meets the Road in terms of the inner game of success, how you get your inner psychology working for you for the long term to ensure the outcomes that you want, your vision for the future, your belief systems that support that vision, the stand that ensures it, your ability to focus, to make sure it happens, your state for in the moment activities, they all go together. And the beauty is, is that the straight line is the outer world execution that allows you to make this all real. See, the problem with a lot of the stuff is that without something like the straight line and gives you that edge, comes a lot of talk and you bring those in. The straight line, what you see and what I saw with all these kids and all the people I trained after those original 12,000 stratinized I trained, right, is that once you learn the straight line, it wakes up that voice in the back of your head that I can do it. I am capable that narrative makes that all important shift. All right, so here we go. Now that we've discussed what you do if your standards are too low, how do you raise them? What happens if you're moving through life or recently you had something happen and you took a hit, it happens all the time and you drop your standards as a result of that? I'll get into this in more detail as you see Sarah ask me this question, why it happens, when it happens, how it happens, but most importantly, what to do about it and the reality of how you come back from that. So let's cut to the live footage right now and I'll see you on the other side and we'll discuss it. So obviously there are people out there who have high standards and maybe they've had a setback and their standards have fallen. So what do you do in that situation if you're that person who's had a setback and you feel that your standards have fallen? Right, well it's a good question. So there's two sides. So there's the side of what you said, high standards, huge setback, me, right, standards fall or high standards, huge setback standards haven't fallen, but what do you do to stop them from falling? Two sides. So this is going to happen to you the way it is. For me, for example, I had these incredibly high standards. I lost everything. Did my standard drop? No, it didn't drop. I maintained my standard and worked 10 times as hard to get where I got again and it's 10 times sweeter the second time around for sure, right? And I'm very fortunate for that. But you're right, very often, I would say more often than not is that someone that had high standards will have a setback in their life and often it's no fault of their own. It could just be a decision that was out of their control, economics, the global financial crisis. I don't, people that had just got buried, like they're just going about their life doing everything. Man, they get hit between the eyes with, as they say, SHIT happens, right? They'll walk it along and bam, there it goes, right? So, and I see people drop their standards. Yes, it happens, right? Let's talk about both situations. So how do you stop it from happening? Well, the first way to prevent your standards from dropping after a setback is to realize this one great truth. That if you drop your standards, if you allow yourself to say, you know what, I have this really high standard, my life no longer reflects that standard. So what happened? What do you feel? You feel pain, you feel discomfort, you feel angst because, you know, I need this, but I don't have it. I felt that for many years and it was awful feeling, right? So one pathway out of that is saying, you know what? It's time to lower my standard and accept life at a lower level now. It's going to accept that because I can't take the pain. The problem with that, and again, this is something that I learned from Dr. Bannler, is that when you drop your standard, it was like, I never experienced myself, but he explained this to me in a very profound way, is that in this competitive world we live in, you know, if you take the route, let's say, having excellent standards, where you just hold yourself to a really high level, doesn't mean you'll get excellent results nowadays. Like, you know, it's like you're just hanging on by a thread. You get excellent standards, but only getting good results. Very common, right? So you say, you know, I can't take this anymore. I got to stop. I'm going to just, let me just go down to good or average standards, right? Well, you know what happens when you have average standards? You're probably going to get poor results and you start going to this self-fulfilling cycle, this nose dive, you're going to rocket-slit the hell with your standards, and before you know it, you all just like, not the same person, and you probably know people like that, who've been sort of step-by-step, you say, what happened to them? They used to be the brightest light in the world. I don't even, I mean, just how they went, just bright and cheery and the zest for life, and you're watching now in their shell, their form of cells. It's that ability to say, you know what, I'm not allowing myself to go there, because I know where this ends up. I cannot drop my standards, because if I do, it's not going to end well for you. That's the first step, to realize it's not possible. You cannot deal with pain in your life by down-regulating what makes you feel pain. That strategy is just not going to end up well, because as you keep down-regulating what you think causes you pain, you lower your standard, and you lower your standards, you're putting worse results, and before you know it, you're living this really incredibly disempowering life. So that's one aspect of the equation. Another side to this, I think that people don't focus this nearly enough, is continuing to educate yourself and grow strong. One of the things that carried me through the negativity, through jail, through losing all my money, was this idea that I was teaching myself this new skill that I was layering on top of my existing skill sets, that was to learn how to write. In other words, I had something I was moving towards. I never stopped moving forward. Forward motion, like Jerry Maguire. Forward motion, forward motion, right? So at some level, it's true that I didn't allow myself to stop and wallow in the misery, not for too long. At least, I admit, of course, I wallowed a little bit, because you have to wallow, because you want to feel some pain, but I used that pain to change my values. So I was focusing on my wallowing as far as I did something really bad here, and I got to change the way I processed the world, because or else I'll end up in the same spot. And I want to live a sustainably wealthy life. I have to think about money and success in a different light, meaning giving value first to people and then monetize that value, right? But on top of that, what I shifted to very quickly in jail was that I'm going to become the best writer in the world, and I put massive hours into perfecting the ability to write. So it's like, keep my mind busy. I know my own psychology, and I think part of this for all of you is knowing what makes you tick. Each of us are a little bit different, but I do believe that an idle mind is the devil's workshop. So what happens with many people is that when they take that hit, when they take that fall, they will sit back and they will ruminate on it, and they will allow themselves to almost become that guy or girl. I refused my standard and my belief systems. Oh, disallowed that. Couldn't happen. So that's another thing. I would stay strong. You want to keep learning, growing yourself. Also, another big one, who do you hang out with? Who do you associate with? Who are your mentors? Who do you look to for guidance? What's your picture of success? What are theirs? My mom used to say to me, if you're lying down with dogs, you'll wake up with fleas. Now she meant it in a different way. Back then, I think it was about kids who smoke and pot, but it has to do with everything in life. It's a true statement. You hang out, associate people with low standards. Guess what? You'll tend to adopt their standards. If you associate with people with high standards, you'll adopt high standards. So who do you associate with? If you take a fall, don't say, well, I don't want to hang out with successful people because they make me feel bad about myself. Because what happens is they isolate. Let me hang out with people who are disempowered. We can all commiserate because misery loves company. So a big one is who are your mentors? Are you continuing to educate and grow yourself? And also, and again, everything always melts down at the end of the game, make sure that when you take that fall, you're very careful with the meaning that you apply to the experiences that led up to it. In other words, are you creating limiting beliefs in your own head? See, typically when you take a fall, like I had to fall in the meat and seafood industry, right, I lost everything. And there was a short time I said, well, you know, maybe I'm just not meant to be an entrepreneur. Now, the ridiculous of that statement, it's beyond ridiculous. I already had six businesses instead of about eight years old, whether it was newspapers, shoveling driveways, magic shows, the beach. I was a born entrepreneur in terms of knowing and believing in the idea of taking risks, right? But here's the thing. I wasn't handed a manual on how to run a business. I didn't go to college for that. I was going to be a doctor. I had a degree in biochemistry. I didn't know the first thing. I go into business. I make all these mistakes. So what happens when the mistakes catch up with me and lose all my money and they take my car away and I'm feeling terrible. You know what that voice says in that disempowered moment when you're in a disempowering state? Maybe I'm just not meant for greatness. That's an incorrect meaning applied through language. So my point is be very, very carefully when you go back and say, okay, let's say I've had a bad experience. I feel like my standards have dropped. Well, you might have to go back to the beliefs module now and maybe run some beliefs changes on any limiting beliefs that you formed as a result of that negative experience that you applied to the wrong meaning. I want you to go back, change that belief, which is easy to do that. You know we can do that, right? So we're going to change that belief and that allows you then to move through life with higher standards and not have dissonance than that. Negative beliefs, high standards, they're not going to go so well. So it's always about having sort of a convergence and an agreement and a gruancy of the inner game. It can't just be about standards. It can't just be about vision. It can't be about state management. What do you call someone who only focuses on state management and walks around all day long? Certainly, we call them an asshole, right? What do you call someone who has only vision? They focus on this great vision, nothing else? Happeless dreamer. What do you call someone who has high standards but none of the other two? An achiever who makes themself crazy and works all day long but never feels good about it. It's about all of them together. So this, the last module standards, this is your long term barometer. I always say, as an ethics and integrity is the gift you give yourself, the gift you give yourselves, right? Standards is the same thing. Having a high standard is the gift that you give yourself. It's your own insurance policy to getting what you want, to achieving and living a wonderful life. And also remember this, if not for you, how about the people that you love and care about? You owe it to them to have a high standard. You know, where do your kids get their standards from? Your kids, they adopt their standards and their beliefs very much from their parents. That's where it starts. It doesn't end there but it starts there. My children have watched me and they've developed really empowering beliefs when it comes to coming back from failure to the power of hard work and diligence, power of just essentially living your life in terms of that you can make your own way. As Hannibal said, you can either find a way or make one versus the world happens to me. If you have a family, you have children, you have a partner, you're doing them no service by allowing your standards to fall or living a life with low standards. You hurt everybody around you like that. When you have someone with high standards and empowering beliefs and a bold vision for their future, you know what you have? A leader who brings everyone else along for the running career, it's greatness in the world. That's what the straight line system is all about because that's the X factor. It's that out of the world skill set that allows you to take all of that and put it into action and actually make the world the way you want it. Bottom line. All right, pretty cool.
